react.answer:
## Building a Quiz App in React

**Mental model:** A quiz walks an array of questions by index, recording the picked answer per question. Selecting locks in a choice and reveals correctness; Next advances; after the last question, show a score derived from the answers. Keep the answers in one array so the score and review are trivial.

### Step 1 — Index + recorded answers
```jsx
const [i, setI] = useState(0);
const [answers, setAnswers] = useState({}); // questionIndex -> chosen index
```

### Step 2 — Pick an answer
Record the choice; don't allow changing once picked (reveal correctness).

```jsx
const pick = (choice) => {
  if (answers[i] != null) return;
  setAnswers((a) => ({ ...a, [i]: choice }));
};
```

### Step 3 — Advance / finish
```jsx
const next = () => setI((n) => n + 1);
const done = i >= questions.length;
```

### Step 4 — Derive the score
```jsx
const score = questions.reduce((s, q, idx) => s + (answers[idx] === q.correct ? 1 : 0), 0);
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Re-pick | Guard once an answer exists for the question. |
| Next before pick | Disable Next until answered. |
| Review | Read `answers` vs `q.correct` per question. |

**Interview tip:** Storing answers keyed by question index (not just a running score) lets you both compute the score *and* build an end-of-quiz review — mention that it generalizes to "back" navigation too.

vue.answer:
## Building a Quiz App in Vue 3

**Mental model:** Walk an array of questions by index, recording the picked answer per question in an object. Picking locks the choice and reveals correctness; Next advances; the final score is *derived* from the recorded answers.

### Step 1 — Index + answers
```vue
<script setup>
import { ref, computed } from 'vue';
const i = ref(0);
const answers = ref({});
</script>
```

### Step 2 — Pick (once) per question
```js
function pick(choice) {
  if (answers.value[i.value] != null) return;
  answers.value = { ...answers.value, [i.value]: choice };
}
```

### Step 3 — Advance / finish
```js
const done = computed(() => i.value >= props.questions.length);
```

### Step 4 — Derive score
```js
const score = computed(() =>
  props.questions.reduce((s, q, idx) => s + (answers.value[idx] === q.correct ? 1 : 0), 0));
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Re-pick | Guard once answered. |
| Next before pick | Disable until answered. |
| Review | `computed` score + per-question read. |

**Interview tip:** Recording answers keyed by index (rather than a bare counter) keeps score *and* review derivable — and makes adding "previous question" navigation a one-liner.
